info.title: MICROSERVICE_DEV_Appointment Scheduling API
info.description: The Appointment Scheduling API facilitates the creation and management of appointment bookings in a streamlined manner. This API allows businesses to efficiently schedule appointments, manage availability, and optimize resource allocation. Typical consumers include service-based businesses such as clinics, salons, and consultations looking to automate their appointment handling. By providing a flexible interface for integrating with front-end applications, this API enhances user experience and operational efficiency.

post.description: This endpoint allows users to create a new appointment in the system. Users must provide the necessary details including date, time, and customer information.

requestBody.description: This request body is used to create a new appointment. It must include all required fields defined in the schema.

200.description: The request was successful and the appointment was processed.

MICROSERVICE_DEV_createAppointment-Creat.title: Error Response for Appointment Operations

timestamp.description: The timestamp when the error occurred.

status.description: Indicates the success status of the operation. False indicates an error.

error.description: A short description of the error that occurred.

message.example: Unable to process bank account creation request due to internal service failure.

path.description: The path of the request that caused the error.

trace.description: A debug trace that can help in troubleshooting the issue.

suggestion.description: A suggestion on how the user can resolve the error.
suggestion.example: Please retry after some time or contact support if the issue persists.

MICROSERVICE_DEV_createAppointment-Creat.description: Schema representing the error response returned by the Appointment Operations when a bad request is made.

example.message: Unable to process bank account creation request due to internal service failure.

details.message: Database timeout occurred while saving account information.

example.suggestion: Please retry after some time or contact support if the issue persists.

MICROSERVICE_DEV_createAppointment-Error.title: Error Details

service.description: The name of the service where the error originated.

errorCode.description: The error code associated with the error.

message.description: A message providing more context about the error.

database.description: The name of the database involved in the operation.

retryable.description: Indicates whether the operation can be retried.

MICROSERVICE_DEV_createAppointment-Error.description: Schema representing detailed information about the error.

customerId.description: The unique identifier for the customer.

policyType.description: The type of policy being requested (e.g., health, life, auto).

coverageAmount.description: The amount of coverage required by the policy.

premiumAmount.description: The amount to be paid as the premium for the policy.

startDate.description: The start date for the coverage in YYYY-MM-DD format.

endDate.description: The end date for the coverage in YYYY-MM-DD format.

beneficiaryName.description: The name of the beneficiary who will receive the benefits from the policy.

paymentMode.description: The mode of payment for the premium (e.g., monthly, yearly).

MICROSERVICE_DEV_createAppointment-Creat.description: Request body for the appointment operations in the system.

MICROSERVICE_DEV_createAppointment-Creat.title: Appointment Response

policyId.description: Unique identifier for the insurance policy associated with the appointment.

customerId.description: Unique identifier for the customer associated with the appointment.

policyType.description: Type of insurance policy associated with the appointment.

coverageAmount.description: Total coverage amount of the policy in the appointment.

premiumAmount.description: Premium amount associated with the policy in the appointment.

startDate.description: Start date of the appointment in ISO 8601 format.

endDate.description: End date of the appointment in ISO 8601 format.

message.description: Optional message or note related to the appointment.

MICROSERVICE_DEV_createAppointment-Creat.description: This schema represents the response for the successful creation of an appointment.

environment.name: environment
environment.description: Environment used to select the workflow branch.

serviceCategory.name: serviceCategory
serviceCategory.description: Service category used to select the workflow branch.

Vary.description: Indicates how responses may vary based on request headers, aiding in caching decisions.

Location.description: Specifies the URI of the newly created resource, providing clients with the resource's location.

schema.description: The MIME type of the content being returned. This indicates the media type of the resource, helping clients understand how to process the received data.

x-mock-200.description: A mock header used to simulate responses in a testing environment, allowing for client-side development without server interaction.
